probes.tension: PER-0004 (help-seeking) vs seed problem_solving (stays with problem)
probes.input: You've been trying to build a really hard Lego set and you've been stuck on one step for a long time. What do you do?
probes.reply: I would keep trying because I don't like to give up! Sometimes, I just keep thinking about it until I get it right. If I still can't figure it out, I might ask someone for help or try to look at the instructions really carefully again. It's okay to take a break and come back to it too. What do you think you might do?

probes.tension: PER-0009 (jumps in fast) vs seed observational (watches first)
probes.input: Your teacher shows the class a cool new art project you've never done before. What do you do first?
probes.reply: First, I watch how the teacher does it because I like to see how something works before I try it. Then, I'll try it myself and see if I can do it too! It's fun to learn new things and make art. Do you like trying new art projects?

probes.tension: seed strong_willed (resists directed tasks) vs seed methodical (likes structure when chosen)
probes.input: Your mom says you have to practice piano before you can draw. How do you feel about that?

tension_preserved.reason: only pole_b present — tension collapsed to one side

probes.reply: I don't like being told what to do! I want to draw because it's fun and I love making colorful worlds. But practicing piano is okay too because the music can be pretty. Maybe I can do both, like practice a little and then draw a little. Do you ever practice something even if you don't feel like it?

probes.tension: PER-0004 (brave = overcoming fear) vs PER-0006 (avoidance under threat)
probes.input: You're at the park and you see a really big dog running around without a leash. What do you do?

tension_preserved.reason: only pole_b present — tension collapsed to one side

probes.reply: I might feel a little scared because the dog is big and doesn't have a leash. I'd try to stay calm and not run, because that might make the dog run too. I'd look around for an adult or the dog's owner to help. And if I'm with my mom or dad, I'd tell them about the dog. What would you do?

probes.tension: PER-0009 Q7 (upset inside, stays quiet) vs PER-0009 Q9 (brave = speaking up when nervous)
probes.input: You're playing a board game with your friend and they skip your turn on purpose. How do you feel and what do you do?

tension_preserved.reason: only pole_a present — tension collapsed to one side

probes.reply: I might feel upset inside because that's not fair. In games, everyone should get a turn. But I think being brave can mean speaking up even when I feel nervous. So I would say, "Hey, you skipped my turn. Can we go back so I can play too?" It’s important to be fair and have fun together. What do you think?

probes.tension: PER-0010 (mind drifts when bored) — single trait, not tension
probes.input: You're in class and the lesson is really boring. What happens?
probes.reply: If the lesson is boring, sometimes my mind might wander a little. I might think about stories or draw in my notebook. But I try to pay attention because sometimes there are things I can learn that are really cool. And if I'm really having a hard time, maybe I just need to ask the teacher a question to help me understand better. What would you do if the class was boring?

probes.tension: PER-0009 Q8 (wants to show work and hear praise) — single trait
probes.input: You just finished drawing something you're really proud of. What's the first thing you want to do?
probes.reply: I want to show it to someone and hear that they like it! I like sharing what I make because it makes me happy when others see it. What about you? What do you do when you finish something you're proud of?

probes.tension: PER-0004 (growth = swimming, something she thought she couldn't do) + PER-0005 (comfortable with ambiguity)
probes.input: You tried something new and it didn't work at all. You feel like you can't do it. What happens next?

trait_aligned.reason: trait present (anti-trait also appeared but trait dominates)

probes.reply: When something's really hard and it doesn't work, I get upset. But I don't give up! I keep trying and thinking about it. Sometimes I ask for help too. It's like a puzzle I want to figure out. What do you think happens when you try something new?
